chief_complaint: A 38-year-old man with Klinefelter syndrome presented with hematemesis, lightheadedness, syncope, and right upper-quadrant pain.
history: The patient had known Klinefelter syndrome (47, XXY) and no prior abdominal surgery. He presented with hematemesis, lightheadedness, syncope, and right upper-quadrant pain. Laboratory testing revealed mild transaminitis, elevated ferritin, and normal bilirubin.
exam: Vital signs not provided. Physical examination findings not provided.
labs: Laboratory testing: mild transaminitis, elevated ferritin, normal bilirubin. Imaging: computed tomography angiography revealed hepatic steatosis and splenomegaly, hepatobiliary iminodiacetic acid scan and ultrasound showed no gallbladder, magnetic resonance cholangiopancreatography confirmed gallbladder agenesis.
